title: On the Necessity of Perspective
author: From: Mark Twain
content: The difference between the right word and the almost right word is the difference between lightning and a lightning bug. Precision is not pedantry; it is clarity.
action: Action: Rewrite your current project’s 'mission statement' into one single, precise sentence.

title: The Paradox of Choice
author: From: Barry Schwartz
content: When we have too many options, we become paralyzed. The joy of a clean, single path is often greater than the anxiety of endless possibility.
action: Action: Remove two 'potential paths' from your current project and commit to one.

title: The Antifragile Principle
author: From: Nassim Taleb
content: Some things benefit from shocks; they thrive and grow when exposed to volatility, randomness, or stressors.
action: Action: Alter your project workflow to be slightly more 'chaotic' on purpose to see how it reacts.

title: The Law of Triviality
author: From: C. Northcote Parkinson
content: Organizations spend the most time discussing the simplest items because everyone can understand them, ignoring the complex, important items.
action: Action: Review your agenda for the next team meeting—is it filled with 'simple' items that hide the 'important' ones?

title: The Bystander Effect
author: From: Social Psychology
content: The more people who know about a problem, the less likely any one person is to solve it. Shared responsibility is often diffused responsibility.
action: Action: If a task has multiple stakeholders, take ownership of one specific sub-task today.
